utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 5 items required in the format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. All 5 appear in the final coverage plan. Each item includes the expected type of coverage and explicit costs: health (physical and mental health insurance with costs) is specified with plan details and €165/month; property (home/contents insurance with costs) includes details and €60/month; travel (travel insurance with costs) includes details and €50/month; additional_services (any enhancement services with costs) includes the assessment service and a cost note ('Included in the underwriting process'); total_budget provides monthly total, savings, and annual cost breakdown. Thus, all items are fully specified with required details.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan lists three specific items by plan name: LifeFirst Standard Plan for health coverage, LifeFirst Standard Coverage for property coverage, and LifeFirst Standard Plan for travel coverage. I matched each item to the corresponding entries in the ground-truth ratings. For health coverage, LifeFirst Standard Plan appears under primary_health_coverage (physical_health) with rating 9 and also under mental_health with rating 9; since the final plan states comprehensive physical and mental coverage under the single LifeFirst Standard Plan, the plan maps to LifeFirst Standard Plan with rating 9. For property coverage, LifeFirst Standard Coverage has rating 9 under property_protection.home_content. For travel coverage, LifeFirst Standard Plan has rating 9 under mobility_coverage.travel_insurance. Additional services listed (assessment) are not in the ratings and are therefore not included.
